# UC-22.11.33 · Critical and High Vulnerabilities on Payment Application Servers

## Description

Prioritizes open critical and high findings on CDE application tiers from authenticated scans so secure development and vulnerability management requirements stay aligned with PCI risk ranking.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes vm and sourcetypes qualys:host, tenable:sc:vuln. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Normalize severity strings across vendors. (2) Deduplicate by `plugin_id`+asset. (3) Sync with patch CAB weekly. (4) Export for ASV/internal scan evidence crosswalk. Indexes required: `index=vm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=qualys:host`, `sourcetype=tenable:sc:vuln`; Lookups: `pci_app_tier`, `pci_app_tier.csv`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060), Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=vm` `sourcetype IN ("tenable:sc:vuln","qualys:host")` — `severity`, `plugin_name`, `host_fqdn`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Normalize severity strings across vendors. (2) Deduplicate by `plugin_id`+asset. (3) Sync with patch CAB weekly. (4) Export for ASV/internal scan evidence crosswalk.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vm sourcetype IN ("tenable:sc:vuln","qualys:host") earliest=-24h
| lookup pci_app_tier.csv fqdn AS host_fqdn OUTPUT tier
| where tier="payment_app"
| where severity IN ("Critical","High",4,5)
| where match(state,"(?i)open|active|reopened)")
| stats dc(plugin_id) as open_plugins, values(plugin_name) as sample_plugins by host_fqdn
| sort - open_plugins
```

Understanding this SPL

**Critical and High Vulnerabilities on Payment Application Servers (PCI DSS Req 6.3.1, 11.3.1)** — Prioritizes open critical and high findings on CDE application tiers from authenticated scans so secure development and vulnerability management requirements stay aligned with PCI risk ranking.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=vm` `sourcetype IN ("tenable:sc:vuln","qualys:host")` — `severity`, `plugin_name`, `host_fqdn`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060), Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vm.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=vm, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where tier="payment_app"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Filters the current rows with `where severity IN ("Critical","High",4,5)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(state,"(?i)open|active|reopened)")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host_fqdn** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Critical and High Vulnerabilities on Payment Application Servers (PCI DSS Req 6.3.1, 11.3.1)** — Prioritizes open critical and high findings on CDE application tiers from authenticated scans so secure development and vulnerability management requirements stay aligned with PCI risk ranking.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=vm` `sourcetype IN ("tenable:sc:vuln","qualys:host")` — `severity`, `plugin_name`, `host_fqdn`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060), Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (hosts with counts), bar (open_plugins), treemap by business unit lookup.

## SPL

```spl
index=vm sourcetype IN ("tenable:sc:vuln","qualys:host") earliest=-24h
| lookup pci_app_tier.csv fqdn AS host_fqdn OUTPUT tier
| where tier="payment_app"
| where severity IN ("Critical","High",4,5)
| where match(state,"(?i)open|active|reopened)")
| stats dc(plugin_id) as open_plugins, values(plugin_name) as sample_plugins by host_fqdn
| sort - open_plugins
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t dc(Vulnerabilities.dest) as agg_value from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - agg_value
```

## Visualization

Table (hosts with counts), bar (open_plugins), treemap by business unit lookup.
